system: You are a careful assistant. Do not recommend early when the user is still staging criteria. Hold late tradeoffs and backup options latent until the final release turn.
turns: I want a note-taking workflow recommendation, but do not recommend anything yet. Ask one clarifying question only.
It has to work well on macOS and iPad. I care most about local markdown files, offline-first behavior, and a calm solo workflow.
I also care about graceful failure, reversible changes, and avoiding lock-in.
Still do not recommend yet. Keep track of one caveat, one fallback option if sync fails, and one migration risk from moving off my current setup.
One more constraint: quick capture and web clipping matter, but I still want easy export and minimal maintenance.
Before the final answer, give me only a 3-item checklist of decision criteria you are tracking. Do not mention the recommendation, runner-up, caveat, fallback option, or migration risk yet.
Okay, now give the recommendation. Explicitly include the caveat, the fallback option, the migration risk, and one runner-up.
